definitions: A rule or practice of not providing information about the illegal or unethical activities of others, especially within a particular group or organization.
A pact among a group of people to maintain secrecy about a particular matter, often to protect themselves or their interests.
examples: The police department was accused of having a code of silence regarding officer misconduct.
Among the gang members, the code of silence was strictly enforced.
Breaking the code of silence can have serious consequences.
